About

Pharmacy meets
strategic thinking

I'm a Third-year B Pharm student building at the intersection of pharmaceutical science and commercial strategy. My work focuses on HTA, HEOR, and market access — areas where most analysts lack the scientific foundation, and most pharmacists lack the business framing.

REAT, my regulatory intelligence platform for Indian pharma manufacturers, is currently in development.

Case Studies

Analytical work on
real market events

01 / 04
Oncology · Market Access

Keytruda's Blockbuster Strategy — Decoding Merck's $25B Franchise

Keytruda generates $29.5B globally yet reaches fewer than 200 of 1,73,487 eligible Indian patients — an effective access rate below 0.3%. A seven-stage patient funnel model identified PMJAY's ₹5L ceiling against a ₹34L annual treatment cost as the primary structural barrier, not diagnosis or infrastructure. Scenario analysis showed only a 90%+ price reduction unlocks PMJAY reimbursement — a precedent Merck has already set in HIV markets.

View Dashboard → View Report → View Data →
02 / 04
Metabolic · Competitive Landscape

GLP-1 Wars — Novo Nordisk vs. Eli Lilly Market Battle

Formulary analysis of the GLP-1 obesity market comparing semaglutide (Wegovy) and tirzepatide (Zepbound) across efficacy, acquisition cost, and cost-efficiency. Zepbound delivered 21% mean body weight loss vs 15% for Wegovy, at $1,060 vs $1,350/month — translating to $50 per 1% weight loss against $90, a 45% cost-efficiency advantage. Recommendation: designate Zepbound as preferred formulary agent.

View Report →
03 / 04
Generics · Regulatory Risk

Montelukast Black Box — How an FDA Warning Reshaped a Generic Market

FDA FAERS audit identified a Proportional Reporting Ratio of 23.75 for neuropsychiatric events linked to montelukast — 11× the signal threshold of 2 — triggering a 2020 Black Box Warning. Analysis mapped the resulting prescribing shift from first-line to second-line use, with indexed forecasting projecting a ~40% market utilization decline vs 2024 baseline by 2028 under the worst-case scenario.

View Report →
04 / 04
HEOR · Market Sizing

Type 2 Diabetes Drug Market — India Landscape Analysis

Cost and access analysis of branded vs generic Metformin 500mg in the Indian market, modelling formulary strategy and savings potential across payer segments. Quantified the price differential, generic substitution economics, and access model implications for India's Type 2 diabetes burden — the world's second-largest diabetic population.

View Data →

Skills

Where science meets
strategy

01
Regulatory Intelligence
Understanding drug approval pathways, post-marketing surveillance frameworks, and regulatory signal detection. Translating regulatory events into commercial risk and opportunity assessments.
02
HEOR & Market Access
Health economics and outcomes research frameworks applied to formulary positioning, payer strategy, and value dossier thinking. Grounded in how clinical evidence translates to reimbursement decisions.
03
Healthcare Data Analytics
Analytical thinking applied to drug market data — prescription trends, pricing dynamics, competitive intelligence, and patient flow analysis. Structured problem decomposition at the core.
04
Business Strategy & Consulting
Structured approach to pharma business problems — market sizing, competitive landscape analysis, go-to-market strategy, and issue-tree driven problem solving with a consulting lens.

Side Project

In Active Development

REAT — Regulatory Intelligence Platform

A B2B SaaS tool being built for Indian pharmaceutical manufacturers to track regulatory submissions, deadlines, and compliance events in real time. Designed to reduce manual regulatory monitoring overhead for mid-size pharma companies navigating CDSCO and global filing requirements.
